_comment: DFToolBench-I tool metadata (Table III of the paper). 12 tools across three categories: P&R, ML&A, QR&A.

tools.name: OCR
tools.description: Extract all visible text from an image using Optical Character Recognition. Returns the recognised text as a plain string.

inputs.name: image_path
inputs.desc: Absolute path to the input image file.

inputs.name: language
inputs.desc: ISO 639-1 language hint for the OCR engine (e.g. 'en', 'zh'). Improves accuracy on multilingual content.

outputs.name: text
outputs.desc: Recognised text content extracted from the image.

tools.name: FaceDetectionTool
tools.description: Detect human faces in an image and return their bounding boxes, landmark coordinates, and detection confidence scores.

inputs.name: image_path
inputs.desc: Absolute path to the input image file.

inputs.name: confidence_threshold
inputs.desc: Minimum detection confidence score in [0, 1]. Detections below this threshold are discarded.

outputs.name: faces
outputs.desc: JSON-encoded list of detected face objects. Each object contains 'bbox' ([x1,y1,x2,y2] in pixels), 'landmarks' (dict of 5-point facial landmarks), and 'score' (float confidence).

tools.name: ObjectDetectionTool
tools.description: Detect and classify objects in an image using a general-purpose object detector. Returns bounding boxes, class labels, and confidence scores.

inputs.name: image_path
inputs.desc: Absolute path to the input image file.

inputs.name: confidence_threshold
inputs.desc: Minimum confidence score in [0, 1] for a detection to be included in the output.

inputs.name: classes
inputs.desc: Optional list of class names (strings) to filter results. If null, all classes are returned.

outputs.name: detections
outputs.desc: JSON-encoded list of detection objects. Each object contains 'label' (class name), 'bbox' ([x1,y1,x2,y2] in pixels), and 'score' (float confidence).

tools.name: DeepfakeDetectionTool
tools.description: Classify whether a face image or video frame has been synthetically generated or manipulated by a deepfake model. Returns a binary label and a probability score.

inputs.name: image_path
inputs.desc: Absolute path to the input image or video frame file.

inputs.name: model_name
inputs.desc: Name of the deepfake detection backbone to use (e.g. 'xceptionnet', 'efficientnet-b4'). Falls back to the default model if not specified.

outputs.name: result
outputs.desc: JSON-encoded object with 'label' ('real' or 'fake') and 'score' (float probability that the image is fake, range 0–1).

tools.name: AnomalyDetectionTool
tools.description: Detect and localise anomalous pixel regions in an image using the PatchGuard model (DINOv2 ViT-S/14 backbone + Discriminator transformer). Returns a binary anomaly mask and the percentage of anomalous pixels.

inputs.name: image_path
inputs.desc: Absolute path to the input image file.

inputs.name: threshold
inputs.desc: Binarisation threshold applied to the continuous anomaly score map (range 0–1). Lower values flag more pixels as anomalous.

outputs.name: result
outputs.desc: JSON-encoded object with 'mask_path' (absolute path to the saved binary anomaly mask PNG) and 'anomaly_pct' (float, percentage of anomalous pixels in range 0–100).

tools.name: FingerprintingTool
tools.description: Extract a perceptual fingerprint (hash or embedding) from an image that can be used to identify its provenance, detect near-duplicate images, or attribute it to a specific generative model.

inputs.name: image_path
inputs.desc: Absolute path to the input image file.

inputs.name: method
inputs.desc: Fingerprinting algorithm to use. Options: 'phash' (perceptual hash), 'dct' (DCT-based hash), 'embedding' (deep feature vector).

outputs.name: fingerprint
outputs.desc: JSON-encoded object with 'method' (string) and 'value' (hex string for hash methods, or JSON array of floats for embedding).

tools.name: DenoiseTool
tools.description: Remove noise from an image to reveal underlying content or to pre-process it for downstream forensic analysis.

inputs.name: image_path
inputs.desc: Absolute path to the noisy input image file.

inputs.name: strength
inputs.desc: Denoising strength in [0, 1]. Higher values apply more aggressive noise removal at the potential cost of fine detail.

inputs.name: output_path
inputs.desc: Absolute path where the denoised image will be saved. If null, a path is derived automatically from image_path.

outputs.name: denoised_path
outputs.desc: Absolute path to the saved denoised image file.

tools.name: SegmentationTool
tools.description: Perform semantic or instance segmentation on an image, producing a labelled mask where each pixel is assigned a class or instance ID.

inputs.name: image_path
inputs.desc: Absolute path to the input image file.

inputs.name: mode
inputs.desc: Segmentation mode: 'semantic' (per-class pixel labels) or 'instance' (per-object instance masks).

inputs.name: output_path
inputs.desc: Absolute path where the segmentation mask image will be saved. If null, derived from image_path.

outputs.name: result
outputs.desc: JSON-encoded object with 'mask_path' (absolute path to the label mask PNG) and 'labels' (list of present class/instance label strings).

tools.name: TextForgeryLocalizerTool
tools.description: Localise regions of text that have been digitally forged or altered in an image (e.g. changed digits, swapped words) and return a binary manipulation mask.

inputs.name: image_path
inputs.desc: Absolute path to the input image (typically a document or sign).

inputs.name: output_path
inputs.desc: Absolute path where the forgery localisation mask will be saved. If null, derived from image_path.

outputs.name: result
outputs.desc: JSON-encoded object with 'mask_path' (absolute path to the binary forgery mask PNG) and 'forged_pct' (float, percentage of text pixels flagged as forged).

tools.name: CopyMoveLocalizationTool
tools.description: Detect copy-move forgery in an image — identifying regions that have been duplicated and pasted elsewhere within the same image — and return source and destination masks.

inputs.name: image_path
inputs.desc: Absolute path to the input image file.

inputs.name: output_dir
inputs.desc: Directory where output mask images are saved. If null, a subdirectory alongside image_path is used.

outputs.name: result
outputs.desc: JSON-encoded object with 'source_mask_path', 'dest_mask_path' (absolute paths to binary PNG masks marking copied and pasted regions), and 'detected' (boolean).

tools.name: SceneChangeDetectionTool
tools.description: Detect abrupt or gradual scene changes (cuts, splices) in a video and return a list of change-point frame indices with associated confidence scores.

inputs.name: video_path
inputs.desc: Absolute path to the input video file.

inputs.name: threshold
inputs.desc: Detection threshold in [0, 1]. Lower values are more sensitive (more candidate change-points).

inputs.name: min_scene_length
inputs.desc: Minimum number of frames between consecutive detected scene changes.

outputs.name: result
outputs.desc: JSON-encoded object with 'change_points' (list of {frame_index, timestamp_s, score} objects) and 'num_scenes' (int).

tools.name: Calculator
tools.description: Evaluate a mathematical expression and return the numeric result. Supports standard arithmetic (+, -, *, /), exponentiation (**), integer division (//), modulo (%), parentheses, and common mathematical functions (sqrt, log, abs, round, min, max, floor, ceil).

inputs.name: expression
inputs.desc: A valid Python-compatible mathematical expression string, e.g. '(3.14 * 5 ** 2) / 2' or 'sqrt(144) + log(100, 10)'.

outputs.name: result
outputs.desc: JSON-encoded object with 'value' (the numeric result as a float or int) and 'expression' (the original expression string).
